text.title: Diary Study Design and Planning
text.summary: Study type selection, design decisions, entry format rules, recruitment targets, and planning checklist for longitudinal diary studies.

section.title: Study Types
section.p: Three diary study types, selected by the nature of the behavior under study:

list.item: Interval-contingent: participants record at fixed times (daily, weekly). Best for: routine behaviors, habit formation, onboarding progression.
Event-contingent: participants record when a specific event occurs (used the product, experienced a problem). Best for: specific interactions, trigger mapping.
Signal-contingent: participants record when prompted at random intervals. Best for: capturing natural moments, reducing recall bias. Requires careful timezone handling per participant.

rule.statement: Cap entry completion time at 5 minutes. Any entry that takes longer causes attrition spikes after day 4.
rule.rationale: Diary fatigue in interval-contingent studies begins around day 7 and accelerates if entry burden is high. The 5-minute cap is the single most important mechanical control for data quality across the study period.

rule.statement: Vary the prompt text daily. Repeated identical prompts degrade response quality after day 4 as participants learn to minimize effort.
rule.rationale: Participants answer what they expect the prompt to ask, not what it actually asks. Prompt variation forces genuine recall each entry.

rule.statement: Recruit 30% over target to absorb 20–40% dropout over 4 weeks. Pay incentives in tranches (signup, mid-study, exit interview).
rule.rationale: Flat one-shot incentive payment increases dropout once the payment is received. Tranche structure maintains engagement through the full study duration.

rule.statement: Run a 3-day pilot with 2 participants before launching the full study.
rule.rationale: Every logistic bug (broken reminder links, confusing entry fields, wrong timezone, missing consent flow) surfaces in a pilot. Fixing post-launch costs 10x the pilot effort.

section.title: Entry Format
section.p: Each entry should collect: timestamp (auto), location/context (structured options), what happened (open text), outcome rating (1–5 scale), problems or frustrations (open text), optional photo or voice note. Limit to 5 required questions maximum.
Weekly micro-interviews (15 min per participant) clarify ambiguous entries while context is fresh. Do not accumulate 4 weeks of entries before starting analysis — code incrementally.

section.title: Planning Checklist
section.p: Before launch: research questions defined, study type and duration decided, entry frequency and format designed, capture platform selected, entry template tested by 2 pilots, incentive structure finalized, ethics review completed (can stall weeks — start early), screening criteria defined, consent flow built.
Recruitment: participants recruited and screened, consent obtained, onboarding session completed, reminder cadence configured, attrition escalation path defined.
